behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request builder/config/taxonomy serialization into the API client payload. A pure unit test of one helper can miss wiring or field-mapping mistakes. Rejected System because behavior text only requires payload content correctness, not real network/OS/subprocess behavior. Rejected Agentic because this does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a capturing fake API client; invoke classification and assert the outbound request contains every configured taxonomy label (no omissions/reordering loss due to serialization).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is deterministic normalization/validation logic on a returned label; one object/function boundary is sufficient. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on mapping invalid label to "unknown", not cross-module state propagation. Rejected Agentic/System because no real model judgment or real infra is needed to expose this bug.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/response-handling path with fixture responses containing out-of-taxonomy labels and assert returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is pure determinism/idempotence of normalization for identical input; no module wiring or I/O is required. Rejected higher levels because behavior text explicitly scopes to normalization step output from identical raw responses.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization repeatedly on the same raw response fixture (table of casing/whitespace variants already fixed) and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls; correctness depends on real model/provider outputs over time. A stub would hide failures tied to real response variation and model-side behavior. Rejected Workflow because this is one agentic decision loop, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute N real calls for identical input, persist raw provider responses and final labels, and assert: whenever raw provider response is unchanged between calls, final label is unchanged; label differences are only allowed when provider response differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/order across tokenizer/budget policy/request dispatcher (must enforce before send). Unit tests of token math alone can miss accidental dispatch despite over-budget input. Rejected System because real network is unnecessary to detect pre-dispatch enforcement order. Rejected Agentic because this checks infrastructure logic, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with real request-construction modules and a spy/fake transport; feed over-budget documents and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs and dispatch behavior matches policy (e.g., reject path makes zero API sends).

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real AI provider token accounting for actual requests; mocks can always return compliant usage and hide tokenizer/model-side drift. Because execution path requires real AI API output metadata, minimum level is agentic. Rejected Workflow because only single model call behavior is validated, not multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real classification calls on representative documents, collect provider-reported token usage metadata, and assert each call's usage is <= configured budget with alert/failure on any overage.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at retry/backoff orchestration boundary between API client, retry policy, and caller contract. In-process fake API returning rate-limit then success can expose real logic failures. Rejected System because real network/OS timing is not required to verify retry decision path. Rejected Agentic because label quality/model judgment is irrelevant.
behaviors.test_description: Use a controllable fake provider that returns rate-limit (e.g., 429/RateLimit) then success; assert module waits/backs off per policy, retries, and ultimately returns successful classification instead of surfacing immediate error.
